item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: take your upvotes and get this thing away from me

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not wildly-original, but original enough for me, and well-executed, and avoids quite a number of treacherous pitfalls, and all that is enough for my vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The extra CSS is unnecessary, however this article, the premise and the imagery creeps me out enough for me to like it. I didn't get to see this article in its original form, but after I read the previous comments, it seems like changing the ending is a right move.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: not sure about the previous ending, but I thought the new ending was pretty solid. There's obviously nothing absolutely profound with this, but I think it's still a solid scp. there are scps already similar to this, like scp-3003, but this still feels like it stands mostly on it's own. I'm gonna give this a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Not really a fan of the concept itself — bugs in the body is a common horror, and the implementation here isn't particularly inspired.

Also, bit of a note: If you've got a big div with a custom background and big font size, it's going to stick out in a reader's field of view and distract them from the passage they're reading further up the page. The larger problem with that particular div is, however, that its contents don't justify the space it takes up on the page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I agree with Ihp. I love the idea, and I want to upvote it, but it's extremely obvious Ussak is infected to anyone with a basic understanding of the anomaly.

EDIT: While this issue has been addressed, this still fails at being good body horror. Blood vessels being used as tunnels for ants is a good premise, however, this SCP fails to create any terrifying imagery with it. I suggest you rewrite it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's being wrong genre savvy, and then there's just plain incompetence. The fact that the Foundation doesn't immediately fumigate Ussak (or at least make them get a freaking CAT scan) means this is a -1 from me.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
They read you smiles, nothing but teeth,
looked at you with pity first, then disbelief.
Let me put myself in your shoes as a puppet loosely strung.

If anyone writes a Ratatouille parody involving this, please PM me so I can plug it here for you.

Critters: Supposedly Spooky | Dysadron | RadioactiveRADS

EDIT: I've changed the ending from "Ussak (and presumably all of Provisional Site-188) gets infected and tries to convince the Foundation that everything is okay while also being incredibly suspicious" to "Provisional Site-188 gets infected, all the personnel flee Valdez, and they haven't been seen since". I'm hoping that this ending is a bit better, since the Foundation is a bit more competent this time around.
